title: SDA director Sandhoo takes on broader Space Force missile warning portfolio
summary: The Pentagon is realigning the Space Development Agency's satellite programs under a new acquisition structure, with SDA director Sandhoo taking on a broader role in the Space Force's missile warning portfolio.

title: Startup challenging satellite industry’s multi-orbit playbook
summary: Contrivian, a startup, challenges the satellite industry's traditional multi-orbit approach, arguing that LEO constellations alone can offer better resilience and speed. The company claims that combining GEO, MEO, and LEO satellites creates networking problems.

title: Portal Space taps Quindar for ground mission support of its maneuvering spacecraft
summary: Portal Space has selected Quindar to provide ground mission support for its upcoming maneuverable spacecraft, which targets the military market. The collaboration aims to enhance the operational capabilities of Portal Space's spacecraft.

title: Vast announces line of high-power satellite buses
summary: Vast, a commercial space station developer, is expanding into satellite manufacturing by introducing a line of high-power satellite buses. This move marks a diversification of the company's capabilities within the space industry.

title: Lynk and Anterix get FCC nod to test satellite D2D for private utility networks
summary: Lynk Global received FCC approval to test satellite direct-to-device (D2D) technology for private utility networks using Anterix's terrestrial spectrum in the U.S. The collaboration aims to enhance connectivity for utility infrastructure.

title: Anderson confirmed as NASA deputy administrator
summary: The U.S. Senate confirmed Matt Anderson as NASA’s deputy administrator on May 18, making him the second-in-command of the space agency.

title: ESA-China SMILE mission lifts off to deliver first global images of Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: The SMILE mission, a joint project between the European Space Agency (ESA) and China, has successfully launched to provide the first global images of Earth's magnetosphere after over a decade of preparation and collaboration.

title: European imaging companies step in to fill warzone gap
summary: European Earth-observation firms are increasing their satellite imagery sharing of conflict zones like Iran and the Gulf region as U.S. companies reduce their coverage. This shift highlights a growing role for European imaging companies in providing critical visual data amid geopolitical tensions.

title: Four NASA payloads to fly on Astrolab’s first lunar rover
summary: Astrolab's first lunar rover will carry four NASA payloads on a mission planned for launch later this year. This collaboration marks a significant step in lunar exploration and commercial space partnerships.

title: Inside Golden Dome’s push to court commercial tech firms and investors
summary: Golden Dome is attempting to attract commercial tech firms and investors to address concerns about affordability, industrial scale, and the viability of commercial space economics for national defense.

title: Lasers in moon craters could create a lunar GPS system
summary: Scientists are exploring the use of lasers in the moon's craters to create a GPS-like navigation system for future lunar missions, including Artemis astronauts. This technology could significantly improve precision in navigating the lunar surface.

title: Worker dies at SpaceX's Starbase in leadup to Starship V3 megarocket launch
summary: A worker died at SpaceX's Starbase facility in South Texas ahead of the planned launch of the Starship V3 megarocket. The incident occurred as the company prepared for the rocket's debut flight.

title: 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' clip reveals how Mando gets his Razor Crest spaceship back (video)
summary: A clip from 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' reveals how The Mandalorian recovers his Razor Crest spaceship. The scene hints at future missions following the recovery.

title: Vast, builder of private space stations, launches line of high-power satellites
summary: Vast, a California-based startup known for its space station plans, has launched a new line of high-power satellites. This marks an expansion of the company's activities beyond its traditional focus on private space stations.

title: See Jupiter glow beside the waxing moon after sunset on May 20
summary: On May 20, Jupiter will appear near the waxing moon after sunset, creating a visible celestial dance. This event offers a chance to observe the gas giant alongside Earth's moon.

title: NASA satellite will test orbital 'gas station' tech to help astronauts reach the moon and Mars
summary: NASA is preparing to test orbital 'gas station' technology with the Liquid Oxygen Flight demonstration (LOXSAT) to advance in-space propellant transfer and storage. This innovation is vital for supporting the Artemis program's missions to the moon and future Mars expeditions.

title: 'Star Wars: Maul – Shadow Lord' confirmed what we already knew — the former Sith has daddy issues
summary: The upcoming Star Wars series 'Maul – Shadow Lord' explores Maul's backstory, revealing his struggles with personal issues. The narrative suggests a deeper emotional layer to the character beyond his Sith past.

title: Galactic starlight will take your breath away | Space photo of the day for May 19, 2026
summary: A new image from the James Webb Space Telescope showcases the galaxy M77 in stunning detail. The photo highlights the breathtaking beauty of the galaxy.

title: As 'The Mandalorian and Grogu" prepare for their close-up, can 'Star Wars' ever feel like a big-screen event again?
summary: The article discusses the return of 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' to the big screen and questions whether 'Star Wars' can regain its former theatrical appeal. It highlights concerns about Disney+ spin-offs potentially diluting the franchise's multiplex experience.

title: Scientists just solved a tricky asteroid-hopping spacecraft riddle
summary: Scientists have developed a new mathematical approach to solve the complex problem of plotting optimal trajectories for asteroid-hopping spacecraft. This breakthrough addresses the challenging Traveling Salesperson problem variant in space exploration.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images Mars’ Huygens Crater
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured an enhanced-color image of Mars' Huygens Crater using its multispectral imager. The image reveals a large double-ring crater and surrounding heavily cratered southern highlands.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Spies Mars’ Wind-Blown Craters During Close Approach
summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft captured images of Mars' wind-blown craters during a close approach on May 15, 2026. The images show streaks formed by wind over impact craters in the Syrtis Major region.

title: Psyche’s High-Resolution View of Mars’ South Pole
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured the highest-resolution view of Mars' water ice-rich south polar cap during a gravity assist flyby. The image reveals details at a scale of 0.7 miles per pixel.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Sees Mars’ South Pole After Flyby
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured its first view of a nearly full Mars during a flyby on May 15, 2026. The spacecraft observed the south polar cap and Valles Marineris canyon system before continuing its journey.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images the Crescent of Mars
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured a crescent image of Mars on May 15, 2026, during a gravity assist flyby using its multispectral imager. This was the final full-planet view before Mars filled the spacecraft's field of view.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Aces Mars Flyby, Targets Metal-Rich Asteroid
summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft successfully completed a Mars flyby on May 15, using the planet's gravity to gain speed and adjust its trajectory toward a metal-rich asteroid. The maneuver was executed without using onboard propellant.

title: Moon-Venus Conjunction
summary: The Moon and Venus appeared in conjunction above the Washington Monument on May 18, 2026, as viewed from NASA Headquarters. This celestial event occurred due to their alignment from Earth's perspective, despite being millions of miles apart in reality.

title: Johnson’s Cindy Evans Prepares Artemis Teams for Lunar Science
summary: NASA's Artemis II crew served as scientific ambassadors during their lunar mission, analyzing and photographing geologic features like impact craters. Johnson’s Cindy Evans played a key role in preparing the team for these scientific tasks.

title: NASA’s New Shock Detectives Project Invites Volunteers to Help Study Solar Wind
summary: NASA's Magnetospheric Multiscale (MMS) mission seeks volunteers for the 'Shock Detectives' project to analyze over a decade of solar wind data. This citizen science effort aims to help researchers identify chaotic and peaceful regions in the data.

title: Farming in Ancient Lake Agassiz
summary: The glacial Lake Agassiz left fertile farmland in southeastern Manitoba due to its silt and clay deposits, which have been farmed since the 19th-century land surveys and remain productive today.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C flight.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Arianespace's Vega C rocket to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy booster, the first stage of Starship, highlighting its ongoing modifications. It is presented as the 'Booster of the Future' by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is advancing planning for Artemis III, a critical crewed mission to the Moon. The mission includes key Human Landing System (HLS) testing.

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12 of its Starship rocket, marking the debut of the next evolution of the vehicle's second stage. The Starship has undergone multiple iterations since its early test flights with SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. The company continued its growth trajectory from 2025.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed the construction of a newly redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. This marks a significant step toward the vehicle's first launch from the upgraded facility.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the CRS-34 mission to the International Space Station (ISS) on its third attempt. The launch follows the docking of Progress MS-34 to the station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: A busy launch week starting May 11 features nine launches across the United States and China, with SpaceX and Chinese missions leading the schedule. The high activity highlights the growing competition and collaboration in the global space industry.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 has completed a Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19 successfully conducted a full-duration, full-thrust static fire test with 33 engines on May 7, 2026.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and selecting materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for ESA's missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh ideas from these early-career scientists.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) to advance space exploration through training, operations, simulations, and public engagement. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for a spacecraft's parachute system, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out.' This test is likely aimed at ensuring the parachute's functionality and reliability under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The mission, launching in 2028, aims to search for signs of life while adhering to strict planetary protection protocols.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has passed rigorous space-like tests, confirming it is on schedule for a launch in early 2027 to search for Earth-like planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, which includes in-person and online sessions. The event will take place at ESA Headquarters in Paris on June 8-9, 2026, preceded by online preparatory sessions in April and May.

title: From Design Reviews to Decision Intelligence: CoLab Rewires Engineering Workflows
summary: CoLab is transforming engineering workflows by improving decision-making processes in engineering production, aiming to enhance efficiency and reduce complexity in product development. The company is shifting from traditional design reviews to a more advanced 'decision intelligence' approach.

title: CSF Forecasts Launch Supply Running Low
summary: The Commercial Spaceflight Federation (CSF) forecasts a potential shortage in launch supply, with annual satellite launches ranging from 6,000 to 230,000, requiring up to 7,000 launches per year depending on the scenario.

title: Vast Launches Satellite Bus Business Line
summary: Vast, a commercial space station company, has launched a new satellite bus business line to sell satellite buses targeting low-cost, high-volume, and high-power missions for communications, Earth observation, and national security programs.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire Solestial
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring Solestial, marking a significant move in the space industry. The acquisition highlights York Space Systems' active M&A strategy this year.

title: Astrolab Unveils Payloads Flying on FLIP Lunar Mission
summary: Astrolab has announced the payloads that will be flying on its upcoming FLIP lunar mission, scheduled for this year. The mission will carry a variety of scientific payloads aboard a lunar rover.

title: Potomac Database Systems Unveils Plans to Amass Lunar Data
summary: Potomac Database Systems has announced plans to collect and manage lunar data, highlighting commercial opportunities beyond traditional mining and infrastructure on the Moon.

title: U-Space Selects Skynopy to Support Multiple Missions
summary: U-Space has selected Skynopy to support multiple missions using its ground-station-as-a-service model. Skynopy's service is gaining significant traction in the space industry.

title: Intuitive Machines Bets on Lunar Comms with Goonhilly Buy
summary: Intuitive Machines acquired UK-based Goonhilly Earth Station and its US subsidiary COMSAT to bolster its lunar communications capabilities. The deal aims to provide integrated deep-space communications and PNT solutions for customers.

title: Iridium Boosts Air Safety Offering With Aireon Acquisition
summary: Iridium has acquired Aireon to enhance its air safety services by integrating space-based VHF communications. This move aims to strengthen Iridium's offerings in aviation safety and tracking.

title: European Space Startups Jockey for ‘Infrastructure’ Financing
summary: European space startups are seeking infrastructure financing as growth-stage capital becomes scarce. The region faces challenges in securing late-stage funding for space ventures.

title: Sandhoo Named SDA Director, PAE for Missile Warning and Tracking
summary: Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo has been officially named as the director of the Space Development Agency (SDA), effective May 11. The SDA is responsible for missile warning and tracking programs.

title: Northrop Grumman’s First MRV Readies for Summer Launch to Expand the Space Servicing Toolkit
summary: Northrop Grumman's SpaceLogistics is preparing to launch its first Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV) this summer for on-orbit refueling and satellite life extension. The MRV aims to expand the company's space servicing capabilities.

title: Eutelsat Network Solutions Adds Get SAT Aero Blade Terminal to Lineup
summary: Eutelsat Network Solutions has expanded its offerings by adding the Get SAT Aero Blade terminal to its lineup, providing more multi-orbit options for government and defense customers. This addition aims to enhance connectivity and service capabilities for U.S. government clients.

title: Sky Perfect JSAT Invests in Astroscale to Partner on On-Orbit Servicing
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT has invested in Astroscale to collaborate on on-orbit servicing initiatives. The partnership aims to advance space debris removal and satellite maintenance technologies.

title: Breaking the Power Constraint in Space With Star Catcher’s Andrew Rush
summary: The article discusses the challenges of power constraints in space engineering, highlighting the role of Star Catcher's Andrew Rush in addressing these issues. It emphasizes how advancements in technology have improved size and weight limitations but power remains a critical constraint.

title: Commercial Space Station Builder Vast Expands Into Satellite Buses
summary: Vast, a company developing the Haven commercial space stations, is expanding into high-power satellite buses. The expansion was announced on Tuesday.

title: Lynk and Anterix Target D2D for Critical Infrastructure In New Testing
summary: Lynk Global and Anterix are collaborating to test direct-to-device (D2D) communications for critical infrastructure sectors using private networks. The partnership aims to enhance connectivity solutions for essential services.

title: Skynopy to Support U-Space Missions With Ground Station Services
summary: Skynopy has secured a contract with U-Space to provide ground station services for the operation of two satellites. U-Space, a Toulouse-based small satellite manufacturer, will rely on Skynopy for these mission-critical services.

title: Space Force Awards Northrop Grumman $398 Million For Enhanced PTS-P
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Northrop Grumman a $398 million contract to develop and build an Enhanced Protected Tactical Satellite Communications-Prototype (PTS-P). The contract is managed by the Space Systems Command (SSC) under the Space Force.

title: iDirectGov Releases WCore Virtualization Tool, Supporting 16 Waveforms
summary: iDirect Government released WCore, a virtualization tool supporting 16 waveforms for rapid deployment, scaling, and management. The tool enhances flexibility in satellite communications.
